{{Quote|Sometimes, I get the feelin' the ''Havoc'' is alive. She sometimes fires before I pull the trigger, an' when we're in hyperspace, I swear her engines sing me to sleep.|[[Nym/Legends|Nym]]|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Havoc''''' was a modification of the experimental [[Scurrg H-6 Bomber/Legends|Scurrg H-6 prototype bomber]]/[[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] and was owned by the [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin]] pirate [[Nym/Legends|Nym]].

==Characteristics==
It had six powerful forward-facing [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], arranged three on each wing, had enough power to tear open all opposing ships that were unfortunate enough to get in the line of fire. A high-powered automated rotating ball turret mounted on the dorsal side of the craft could shoot at targets by itself when the ship's pilot was otherwise occupied.<ref name="SF guide" /> After the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], [[Jinkins]] added a bank of [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], [[Proximity mine/Legends|proximity mines]], and [[cruise missile]]s.<ref name="SW:JF" />

[[File:Havoc-swjsf.jpg|thumb|left|180px|''Havoc''{{'s}} ventral surface]]
This was also supplemented with a charged bomb, called the plasma scourge, which was even deadlier.<ref name="SF guide" /> This generator was totally modular, allowing its safe removal for use in other craft when necessary.

The ''Havoc'' also possessed a small cargo hold, which Nym utilized when delivering [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbines]].

The original Scurrg H-6 bomber design required a minimum crew of five: one pilot, a gunner for the turret, a secondary gunner, a navigator, and a demolitions expert in charge of the bombs. However, the intensive modifications wrought by Jinkins allowed Nym to fly the starfighter alone, with only two [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] as backup.

==History==
===Birth of a fighter===
[[File:HavocNym.png|thumb|left|270px|Nym named his Scurrg H-6 bomber, the ''Havoc''.]]
The ''Havoc'' was a one-of-a-kind prototype constructed by the [[Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated/Legends|Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated]]. The Nubians hoped to sell an entire line of these formidable starfighters to the [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], but the pacifistic world rejected them as being too heavily armed.

The lead designer and engineer for the ''Havoc'', a [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] named Jinkins, attempted fruitlessly to convince the Collective that the Scurrg H-6 model still had hope. After a few years of worthless lobbying, the Collective placed the bomber into indefinite storage.

The intricate design on the ''Havoc''{{'s}} wings was the logo of the pirate band, which established a [[Nym's Base|permanent base]] on the planet [[Lok/Legends|Lok]].<ref name="SW:SF" />

===Fighting the Federation===
[[File:Havoc-SWSF.jpg|thumb|right|200px|''Havoc''{{'s}} dorsal surface]]
He used it until he was captured by the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Vana Sage]]. He later escaped with the help of a disguised Jinkins and [[Kole]].<ref name="Cell">{{Tales|7|Single Cell}}</ref> He continued to use it in raids against [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] shipping in the [[Karthakk system]].<ref name="SW:SF" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC Naboo">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] happens in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> the Federation took control of Nym's base, and Nym loaned his services and his starfighter to the [[Naboo Resistance]]. Nym, Sage, and the Naboo pilot [[Rhys Dallows]] teamed up to [[Battle of Eos (Invasion of Naboo)|attack]] a Federation factory on [[Eos]], then stole armaments in a [[Skirmish over Naboo|dogfight]] over Naboo. Nym and Sage then joined the [[Battle of the Naboo resistance base|defense]] of a [[Naboo resistance base]]. After the invasion was finished, Nym departed the planet to see to his crew.<ref name="SW:SF" />

===Ending an occupation===
{{Expandsect}}
Almost [[22 BBY/Legends|10 years later]],<ref name="NEC Geonosis">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Nym was still flying the ''Havoc'' against the Trade Federation's [[Occupation of Karthakk|occupation]] of the [[Karthakk system]]. He and his crew joined the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]. The Revenants attacked [[Spacestation 1138]] to free prisoners, then fought [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]] on [[Maramere]]. The ''Havoc'' was also used in the multistage [[battle of Nod Kartha]]. Nym then flew his starfighter to [[Battle of Lok (22 BBY)|free]] his base from the Trade Federation.<ref name="SW:JF" />

During the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] nearly 22 years later, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] created a model of the infamous starfighter, which were later sold during [[Empire Day/Legends|Empire Day]] by various Imperial organizers of the festivities.<ref name="Galaxies">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=item|Havoc Starfighter}}</ref> Nym still owned the original vessel during this time.

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Havoc'' is a playable craft in the [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] series.
